About: It is about the Paris-Menton rail line.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Exposition Universelle of 1900.
BuiltIn: It was built in the gare de Lyon.
ConsistsOf: It consists of panels depicting 20 major cities and sites.
CreatedBy: It was created by Jean-Baptiste Olive.
It was created by Jean-Paul Letellier.
CreatedFor: It was created for the renovation of the station.
CreatedTo: It was created to liven up a non-descript transit area.
It was created to reflect and encourage the growing interest in leisure travel.
HasA: It has repeated use of frames.
It has imitations of pillars done in painted plaster.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its length of 100 meters.
It is characterized by being painted on linen canvases.
It is characterized by the repeated use of frames.
It is characterized by imitations of pillars done in painted plaster.
It is characterized by depicting cities with their characteristic geographical and architectural elements.
It is characterized by names indicated above the painting.
It is characterized by anachronisms in the 1980 panels.
It is characterized by contemporary cars and people in modern dress sharing the landscape with older rural images.
HeldIn: It was held in the gare de Lyon.
IsA: It is a work commissioned as part of the renovation of the station.
It is a publicity for tourism to the region.
KnownAs: It is known as the "fresco gallery".
It is known as the "hall of lost steps".
KnownFor: It is known for depicting 20 major cities and sites accessible via the Paris-Menton rail line.
LocatedIn: It is located in the gare de Lyon.
It is located in the 12th arrondissement of Paris.
It is located in the ticketing hall of the gare de Lyon.
It is located on the hall’s southwest wall.
It is located along Concourse 1 of the historic main rail station.
OccurredBetween: It occurred between April 1980 and March 1981.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the early twentieth century.
PartOf: It is part of the gare de Lyon.
ProducedBy: It was produced by the Compagnie des chemins de fer de Paris à Lyon et à la Méditerranée.
It was produced by the national rail company Société Nationale de Chemin de Fer.
UsedAs: It is used as publicity for tourism to the region.
UsedFor: It is used for depicting major cities and sites.
UsedIn: It is used in the ticketing hall of the gare de Lyon.
UsedTo: It is used to represent 6 Mediterranean cities.
It is used to represent the cities of Lyon, Avignon, the castle of Tarascon and Nîmes.
It is used to represent Venice.
It is used to represent the Paris-Lyon section of the rail line.
It is used to represent the main destinations and tourist sites accessible by train from the gare de Lyon.
